title: SplitFaceFeatures Object
description: Collection that provides access to all of the existing split face features in a component and supports the ability to create new split face features.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Creates a new split face feature.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: createInput
methods.description: Creates a SplitFaceFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the split face you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the SplitFaceFeatureInput object.   A newly created SplitFaceFeatureInput object defaults to creating a split face feature using the "Split with Surface" option. You can use functions on the SplitFaceFeatureInput object to define a different type of split type.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Function that returns the specified split face feature using an index into the collection.

methods.name: itemByName
methods.description: Function that returns the specified split face feature using the name of the feature.

properties.name: count
properties.description: The number of split face features in the collection.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

related_samples.name: Split Face Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new split face feature.

add.title: SplitFaceFeatures.add Method
add.description: Creates a new split face feature.
add.return_description: Returns the newly created SplitFaceFeature object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: input
parameters.description: A SplitFaceFeatureInput object that defines the desired split face feature. Use the createInput method to create a new SplitFaceFeatureInput object and then use methods on it (the SplitFaceFeatureInput object) to define the split face.

related_samples.name: splitFaceFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the splitFaceFeatures.add method by spliting a face with another intersecting face.

related_samples.name: Split Face Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new split face feature.

classType.title: SplitFaceFeatures.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

createInput.title: SplitFaceFeatures.createInput Method
createInput.description: Creates a SplitFaceFeatureInput object. Use properties and methods on this object to define the split face you want to create and then use the Add method, passing in the SplitFaceFeatureInput object. A newly created SplitFaceFeatureInput object defaults to creating a split face feature using the "Split with Surface" option. You can use functions on the SplitFaceFeatureInput object to define a different type of split type.
createInput.return_description: Returns the newly created SplitFaceFeatureInput object or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: facesToSplit
parameters.description: Input the faces to be split. The collection can contain one or more faces from solid and/or open bodies.

parameters.name: splittingTool
parameters.description: Input entity(s) that defines the splitting tool. The splitting tool can be a single entity or an ObjectCollection containing solid and/or open bodies, construction planes, faces, or sketch curves that partially or fully intersect the faces that are being split.

parameters.name: isSplittingToolExtended
parameters.description: A boolean value for setting whether or not the splittingTool is to be automatically extended (if possible) so as to completely intersect the faces that are to be split. This is only used when the split type is "split with surface" which is the default type when a new createInput is created. Use functions on the returned SplitFaceFeatureInput to define a different type of split type.

related_samples.name: splitFaceFeatures.add
related_samples.description: Demonstrates the splitFaceFeatures.add method by spliting a face with another intersecting face.

related_samples.name: Split Face Feature API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new split face feature.

item.title: SplitFaceFeatures.item Method
item.description: Function that returns the specified split face feature using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

itemByName.title: SplitFaceFeatures.itemByName Method
itemByName.description: Function that returns the specified split face feature using the name of the feature.
itemByName.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if the specified name was not found.

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: The name of the feature within the collection to return. This is the name seen in the timeline.

count.title: SplitFaceFeatures.count Property
count.description: The number of split face features in the collection.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = splitFaceFeatures_var.count

isValid.title: SplitFaceFeatures.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = splitFaceFeatures_var.isValid

objectType.title: SplitFaceFeatures.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():
